season4EpisodeGuide.intro: Season 4 includes 10 episodes and closes the main Harry Vanderspeigle storyline.

storyRecapAndEndingExplained.title: Resident Alien Netflix Story Recap and Ending Explained
storyRecapAndEndingExplained.intro: Resident Alien follows Harry Vanderspeigle from crash-landed invader to unlikely protector of Patience, Colorado. This recap separates spoiler-light setup points from finale-level reveals.

steps.title: The Setup
steps.description: An alien crash-lands near Patience, kills the real Harry Vanderspeigle, and hides in plain sight while trying to finish his mission.

steps.title: Season 1: Harry Starts Changing
steps.description: While posing as town doctor, Harry gets pulled into local mysteries and begins changing through his bond with Asta, Max, and Patience.

steps.title: Season 2 and 3: The Greys and Moonbase Arc
steps.description: The Greys become a direct threat, Bridget enters the center of the story, and a Mantid identity takeover creates a major cliffhanger.

steps.title: Season 4: Return, Final Threat, and Ending Meaning
steps.description: Harry returns to Patience, faces a final mission-linked alien threat, and ends the series by choosing empathy, sacrifice, and protection over destruction.

reviewsAndRatings.title: Resident Alien Netflix Reviews and Ratings Snapshot
reviewsAndRatings.intro: Resident Alien Netflix is strongly rated across major review platforms, with high critic approval and durable audience support.

items.label: Rotten Tomatoes
items.note: Average Tomatometer from 44 critic reviews, with an 87% audience score from 1,000+ ratings.

items.label: Season-Level Critic Scores
items.note: Visible season listings show consistent critic support across the full run.

items.label: Metacritic
items.note: Metascore 70 from critics and user score 7.0 from 188 ratings, both generally favorable.

items.label: IMDb
items.note: Based on roughly 87K user ratings, reflecting broad viewer approval.

items.label: Review Takeaway
items.note: Best fit for viewers who want alien mystery, small-town comedy, and emotional sci-fi character arcs.

filmingLocationsGuide.title: Resident Alien Netflix Filming Locations and Patience Guide
filmingLocationsGuide.intro: Patience, Colorado is fictional, while Resident Alien Netflix production was primarily built in British Columbia, Canada.

items.description: Patience is the fictional mountain town at the center of Harry’s story and is not a real Colorado municipality.
Ladysmith on Vancouver Island serves as the strongest real-world stand-in for Patience exterior street scenes.
Vancouver-area production and soundstages handle core interiors including civic buildings, bar, diner, and home sets.

items.place: Britannia Beach and Howe Sound Area
items.description: Used for the lakeside cabin exterior feel, while major interior cabin scenes were produced as controlled studio sets.

items.place: Sea-to-Sky Corridor and Pemberton Ice Cap
items.description: Mountain and glacier-style landscapes from British Columbia shape the show’s dramatic wilderness look.

comicSourceMaterialGuide.title: Resident Alien Netflix Comic Source Material Guide
comicSourceMaterialGuide.intro: Resident Alien Netflix is based on the Dark Horse comic by Peter Hogan and Steve Parkhouse, then expanded into a broader ensemble TV adaptation.

items.name: 2013 Comic Origins
items.description: Resident Alien began as a Dark Horse comic about an alien hiding as Dr. Harry Vanderspeigle in a small town.

items.name: Core Creators
items.description: Writer Peter Hogan and artist Steve Parkhouse are the foundational creative team behind the comic line.

items.name: Volume 8 and Ongoing Story
items.description: The Book of Life continued the collected story in January 2025, followed by The Book of Changes as a new arc.

items.name: How TV Differs from the Comic
items.description: The show keeps the alien-doctor premise but expands the ensemble, mythology, and emotional arc around Harry, Asta, and Patience.

items.name: Reading Path
items.description: Collected comic volumes remain the most direct way to follow the quieter source-material version of Harry’s mystery.
